name: Agrotoken (SOYA / CORA / WHEA)

custodian.name: Grain stored in licensed warehouses; warehouse receipts as backing

oracle.name: Buenos Aires/Rosario grain exchange spot prices

regulator.name: Argentina CNV (securities regulator, informal engagement); Brazil CVM partnership for cross-border

summary: First major agricultural commodity tokenization platform in Latin America. SOYA (soybeans), CORA (corn), and WHEA (wheat) are each 1:1 backed by physical grain stored in licensed Argentine warehouses. Farmers tokenize harvest as collateral to access stablecoin liquidity, hedge inflation (Argentina's >100% annual rate), and transact with input suppliers. Partnerships with Visa (debit card), Santander, and BBVA for fiat ramps. Represents the most concrete example of RWA addressing real economic problems in high-inflation emerging markets.

sources.title: Reuters: Argentina farmers turn to grain-backed stablecoins amid peso crisis

underlying_asset: Soybeans (SOYA), corn (CORA), wheat (WHEA) stored in licensed Argentine warehouses with verifiable warehouse receipts.
reserve_composition: 1:1 backed — each token represents 1 tonne of stored grain. Stored at warehouses licensed under Argentina's Ley de Warrants.
secondary_market: Listed on Bitso, Buenbit (LatAm exchanges); usable as on-chain collateral for stablecoin loans.
oracle_provider: Buenos Aires Grain Exchange and Rosario Grain Exchange spot price feeds.
redemption_mechanism: Token holder can redeem against physical grain at warehouse, or convert to fiat/stablecoin at exchange price.
upgrade_authority: Agrotoken SA management; no decentralized governance.

timeline.event: Agrotoken launches SOYA, CORA, WHEA in Argentina
Visa partnership for tokenized commodity-backed cards
Brazil expansion announced via CVM regulatory sandbox
